authoritarianism_democracy.name: Authoritarianism ↔ Democracy
authoritarianism_democracy.leftDesc: Strong concentration of power; limited participatory institutions.
authoritarianism_democracy.rightDesc: Broad political participation and competitive elections.

zones.label: Tyranny / dictatorship
zones.description: Rule from a single centre with no meaningful checks on power.

zones.label: Authoritarian regime
zones.description: Institutions exist formally, but opposition is weak.

zones.label: Hybrid regime
zones.description: A mix of democratic and authoritarian practice.

zones.label: Representative democracy
zones.description: Elections and parliament are the core of legitimacy.

zones.label: Direct democracy
zones.description: Maximum direct citizen participation in decisions.

centralization_federalization.name: Centralization ↔ Federalization
centralization_federalization.leftDesc: Decisions and resources concentrated in the capital.
centralization_federalization.rightDesc: Regions and constituent units have substantial autonomy.

zones.label: Unitary state
zones.description: A single administrative chain of command.

zones.label: Devolution
zones.description: Gradual transfer of powers downward.

zones.label: Federation
zones.description: Clear separation between levels of government.

zones.label: Confederation
zones.description: A union with high autonomy for members.

monarchy_republic.name: Monarchy ↔ Republic
monarchy_republic.leftDesc: Inherited supreme authority and royal symbolism.
monarchy_republic.rightDesc: Elected bodies and no hereditary rule.

zones.label: Absolute monarchy
zones.description: The monarch is the primary source of laws.

zones.label: Constitutional monarchy
zones.description: Parliament and law constrain the monarch.

zones.label: Hybrid form
zones.description: Features of monarchy and republic in balance.

zones.label: Parliamentary republic
zones.description: Government answers to parliament.

zones.label: Presidential republic
zones.description: Strong executive power of the president.

theocracy_secularism.name: Theocracy ↔ Secularism
theocracy_secularism.leftDesc: Religious law and clergy shape the foundations of state.
theocracy_secularism.rightDesc: Neutral state on religion; pluralism among faiths.

zones.label: Theocracy
zones.description: Theological norms define what is lawful.

zones.label: State religion
zones.description: Official cult and church privileges.

zones.label: Religion in public life
zones.description: Strong presence of religion in symbols and norms.

zones.label: Secular state
zones.description: Separation of spheres and equality of denominations.

police_state_civil_liberties.name: Police state ↔ Civil liberties
police_state_civil_liberties.leftDesc: Heavy surveillance, limits on privacy and assembly.
police_state_civil_liberties.rightDesc: Strong freedoms of speech, association, and judicial protection.

traditionalism_progressivism.name: Traditionalism ↔ Progressivism
traditionalism_progressivism.leftDesc: Stable norms, community bonds, religion as anchor.
traditionalism_progressivism.rightDesc: Reforms, minority rights, and evolving social norms.

estate_meritocracy.name: Estate hierarchy ↔ Meritocracy
estate_meritocracy.leftDesc: Status and access to power tied to birth and class.

planned_market.name: Planned ↔ Market economy
planned_market.rightDesc: Prices and competition as the primary regulator.

zones.label: Command economy
zones.description: Plans and allocation from above.

zones.label: Regulated planned economy
zones.description: Planning with space for market elements.

zones.label: Mixed economy
zones.description: State and market share key levers.

zones.label: Market with regulation
zones.description: Markets dominate; regulation is selective.

zones.label: Free market
zones.description: Minimal barriers and interference.

protectionism_free_trade.name: Protectionism ↔ Free trade
protectionism_free_trade.leftDesc: Tariffs and quotas to shield domestic sectors.
protectionism_free_trade.rightDesc: Minimal barriers for imports and exports.

agrarian_industrial.name: Agrarian ↔ Industrialization
agrarian_industrial.leftDesc: Farmland as the backbone of the economy.

zones.label: Agrarian society
zones.description: Most people work land and natural cycles.

zones.label: Agrarian–industrial
zones.description: Transitional sectors and urbanization.

zones.label: Industrial society
zones.description: Factories, infrastructure, mass production.

zones.label: Post-industrial society
zones.description: Services, IT, science, global value chains.

state_private_property.name: State property ↔ Private property
state_private_property.leftDesc: State and communal assets at the core.
state_private_property.rightDesc: Private titles and liquid asset markets.

militarism_pacifism.name: Militarism ↔ Pacifism
militarism_pacifism.leftDesc: Armed forces wield major political leverage.

sovereignty_integration.name: Sovereignty ↔ Integration
sovereignty_integration.leftDesc: Full control over laws and borders.
sovereignty_integration.rightDesc: Pooled sovereignty in unions and supranational bodies.

zones.label: Full independence
zones.description: No binding obligations; national sovereignty first.

zones.label: Alliance relations
zones.description: Treaties and coordination without surrender of sovereignty.

zones.label: Confederal structures
zones.description: Shared institutions with divided competences.

zones.label: Supranational union
zones.description: Deep integration of laws and policies.

xenophobia_openness.name: Xenophobia ↔ Openness
xenophobia_openness.leftDesc: Fear of outsiders; rigid barriers to newcomers.
xenophobia_openness.rightDesc: Migration and exchange as norms and strengths.

loyalism_opposition.name: Loyalism ↔ Opposition
loyalism_opposition.leftDesc: Backing current elites and lawful institutions.
loyalism_opposition.rightDesc: Critical of the regime and intent on change.

reformism_revolution.name: Reformism ↔ Revolutionary stance
reformism_revolution.rightDesc: Sharp rupture with the previous order acceptable.

zones.label: Conservatives
zones.description: Resistance to change; preserving the status quo.

zones.label: Reformists
zones.description: Evolution of rules and institutions.

zones.label: Radical reformists
zones.description: Rapid reforms without violent coup.

zones.label: Revolutionaries
zones.description: Violent overturn of the system as an acceptable goal.

zones.label: Official party
zones.description: Fully legal status and access to institutions.

zones.label: Semi-legal movement
zones.description: Partial legitimacy; grey-zone activity.

zones.label: Illegal movement
zones.description: Banned activity without deep secrecy.

zones.label: Underground organisation
zones.description: Strict conspiracy and covert networks.

conformism_radicalism.name: Conformism ↔ Radicalism
conformism_radicalism.rightDesc: Strident rhetoric; low tolerance for compromise.

collectivism_individualism.name: Collectivism ↔ Individualism
collectivism_individualism.rightDesc: Personal autonomy and voluntary choice prized.

egalitarianism_elitism.name: Egalitarianism ↔ Elitism
egalitarianism_elitism.leftDesc: Even access and role distribution valued.
egalitarianism_elitism.rightDesc: Leadership layer and hierarchical competence norms.

nonviolence_armed_struggle.name: Nonviolence ↔ Armed struggle
nonviolence_armed_struggle.rightDesc: Weapons and coercion may be legitimised strategically.

zones.label: Principled non-violence
zones.description: No use of force even in retaliation.

zones.label: Civil resistance
zones.description: Boycotts, strikes, demonstrations.

zones.label: Coercive actions
zones.description: Conflicts short of systemic war.

zones.label: Armed struggle
zones.description: Guerrilla or military line as strategy.

publicity_conspiracy.name: Publicity ↔ Conspiracy
publicity_conspiracy.leftDesc: Open campaigns and visible media fronts.
publicity_conspiracy.rightDesc: Cells and minimal trace in public.

diplomacy_confrontation.name: Diplomacy ↔ Confrontation
diplomacy_confrontation.rightDesc: Hard pressure and frontal challenge to rivals.

zones.label: Mass movement
zones.description: Tens of thousands of supporters and activists.

zones.label: Broad-based party
zones.description: Large but manageable structure.

zones.label: Cadre party
zones.description: Professional core and discipline.

zones.label: Order / inner circle
zones.description: Elite fellowship with rituals and filters.

secular_religious.name: Secularity ↔ Religiosity (ideological)
secular_religious.rightDesc: Vows, dogma, and ritual at the ideological core.

class_based_transclass.name: Class‑bound ↔ Cross‑class
class_based_transclass.rightDesc: Alliances spanning strata "for the common cause".
